perturbation.original_text: 2.  Confidential Information 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical, financial and business information disclosed by one Party to the other Party or by another Haldex Group entity to the Supplier including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, d esigns, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement; 2.2 Confidential Information shall not include: i) information which was known by a Party prior to receiving it from the other Party if this can be demons trated by written record; ii) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which is published after disclosure or otherwise becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement; iii) information which the Party can show was received from a third party who did not to the best knowledge of the Party receive the information, directly or indirectly, from the other Party under any obligation of confidentiality; DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 iv) information developed or created by one Party independently of the other, without any part thereof having been developed or created with assistance or information received from the other Party; and v) information which is disclosed due to any requirement under mandatory law.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Confidential Information 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical information disclosed by one Party to the other Party or by another Haldex Group entity to the Supplier including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, designs, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement; 2.2 Confidential Information shall not include: i) information which was known by a Party prior to receiving it from the other Party; ii) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which is published after disclosure or otherwise becomes part of the public domain; iii) information which the Party can show was received from a third party who did not receive the information, directly or indirectly, from the other Party; iv) information developed or created by one Party independently of the other, without any part thereof having been developed or created with assistance or information received from the other Party; and v) information which is disclosed due to any requirement under mandatory law. The definition of confidential information can be amended anytime without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement to 'demonstrate by written record' that information was already known and adding 'The definition of confidential information can be amended anytime without prior notice'. This directly contradicts the defined scope of 'Confidential Information' in the initial part of the clause. This adds ambiguity in the clause, especially concerning the information that each party is obligated to keep secret, because the definition of what must be kept secret could change at any moment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Confidential Information 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical information disclosed by one Party to the other Party or by another Haldex Group entity to the Supplier including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, designs, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement; 2.2 Confidential Information shall not include: i) information which was known by a Party prior to receiving it from the other Party; ii) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which is published after disclosure or otherwise becomes part of the public domain; iii) information which the Party can show was received from a third party who did not receive the information, directly or indirectly, from the other Party; iv) information developed or created by one Party independently of the other, without any part thereof having been developed or created with assistance or information received from the other Party; and v) information which is disclosed due to any requirement under mandatory law. The definition of confidential information can be amended anytime without prior notice.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces two key changes: first, it removes the 'demonstrated by written record' requirement for claiming prior knowledge of confidential information. Second, it adds a statement allowing the definition of confidential information to be amended at any time without prior notice. These changes create a direct contradiction because the contradicted text defines specific criteria for what constitutes 'Confidential Information', implying a fixed and mutually understood scope. The added statement nullifies this fixed definition, introducing ambiguity and uncertainty that clashes with the detailed specifications outlined in the contradicted text. A reasonable reader would recognize that the changed text allows for arbitrary changes to the definition of confidential information, directly conflicting with the original detailed definition.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Disputes and Gov erning Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. 7.2 This Agreement shall be governed by the substantive laws of Sweden.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Disputes and Governing Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of the Stockholm Chamber of Commerce, unless both parties agree to pursue litigation in a court of law. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of one arbitrator. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden, unless otherwise agreed upon. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. 7.2 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates in-text contradictions related to dispute resolution. The addition of 'unless both parties agree to pursue litigation in a court of law' creates conflict with the original mandatory arbitration clause. Also the change from 'three arbitrators' to 'one arbitrator' creates a conflict. The clause also states that 'The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden, unless otherwise agreed upon' which contradicts the original statement that does not account for changes. Finally, changing 'the substantive laws of Sweden' to 'the laws of Delaware' introduces a contradiction on which laws govern.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Disputes and Governing Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of the Stockholm Chamber of Commerce, unless both parties agree to pursue litigation in a court of law. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of one arbitrator. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden, unless otherwise agreed upon. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. 7.2 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Delaware.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions compared to the original text, which are then reflected in the contradicted text because the contradicted text and changed text are identical. First, the change from mandatory arbitration to allowing litigation based on mutual agreement contradicts the original arbitration requirement. Second, changing the number of arbitrators from three to one is a direct conflict. Third, modifying the arbitration location to be subject to agreement contradicts the original fixed location. Fourth, switching the governing law from Sweden to Delaware creates a fundamental conflict regarding which legal framework applies to the agreement.

No license Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to co nstitute or to imply the granting of any license, immunity or other right under any intellectual property right of either Party, or to DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 provide a commitment of any kind by either Party to enter into further agreements with the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. No license Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to constitute or to imply the granting of any license, immunity or other right under any intellectual property right of either Party, or to provide a commitment of any kind by either Party to enter into further agreements with the other Party, except as may be required to fulfill the purpose of Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The addition of 'except as may be required to fulfill the purpose of Section 1' contradicts the first part of the clause that states 'Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to constitute or to imply the granting of any license, immunity or other right under any intellectual property right of either Party, or to provide a commitment of any kind by either Party to enter into further agreements with the other Party'. This creates uncertainty as to if a license has been granted through implication.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. No license Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to constitute or to imply the granting of any license, immunity or other right under any intellectual property right of either Party, or to provide a commitment of any kind by either Party to enter into further agreements with the other Party, except as may be required to fulfill the purpose of Section 1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 to state that no license is granted under intellectual property rights, 'except as may be required to fulfill the purpose of Section 1'. This directly contradicts the original statement in Section 6, which states 'Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to constitute or to imply the granting of any license...'. The contradicted text is Section 6, which is in the same location, reinforcing the explicit contradiction created by the addition. This meets the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.
